web.markdown:
![OpenAI - ChatGPT Plus](https://getsby.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/OpenAI-ChatGPT-Plus.webp)

Virtual Card to pay for OpenAI API and ChatGPT Plus
===================================================

###### [Order your Virtual Card today](https://getsby.com/en/my-account/)

![Author Image](https://getsby.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/Getsby-Blog-Team-Logo-e1731662729941.png)[Getsby Team](https://getsby.com/)
 | January 26, 2026

Virtual Mastercard Card for OpenAI API and ChatGPT Plus
-------------------------------------------------------

Open a paid plan for OpenAI API’s or ChatGPT Plus or Pro today using the [Virtual Green Card](https://getsby.com/en/virtual-mastercard/virtual-green-card/)
 from Getsby. The Green Card is a reloadable Debit Mastercard that can be linked to pay for your ChatGPT Plus subscription ($20/month) or OpenAI API usage (billed per token).

Unfortunately, not everybody has access to a credit card and PayPal is not accepted payment method, making our cards a great alternative. Green Cards can easily be added to your account without you worrying if the card will be rejected.

Reloadable Virtual Card
-----------------------

The Green Card stands out as the perfect payment method for securing your $20/month ChatGPT Plus or $200/month Pro subscription, while also serving developers efficiently when acquiring API tokens — thanks to its practical monthly spending limits. Stay in control by topping up your balance anytime with a credit card or instant bank transfer.

For security reasons, please note that you can freeze/unfreeze your card at any time. This gives you complete control to prevent unwanted charges or hidden fees. As a bonus for using the Green Card, we plant a tree 🌳 for every € 200 you spend with your card.

###### [Get your Virtual Green Card](https://getsby.com/en/my-account/)

![Virtual Green Card](https://getsby.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/12/Virtual-Green-Card_Tekengebied-1-e1652647275960.png)

What is ChatGPT?
----------------

OpenAI and ChatGPT have been booming ever since the official launch on November 30th 2022. People have been exploring the possibilities, but what is it exactly? ChatGPT is an AI Chatbot initially developed by [Open AI](https://openai.com/)
 to be used for customer service. The chatbot is trained to respond like support teams would on customer questions. However, Chat GPT is also used for other solutions, like writing code, blog posts, stories and translating.

Is ChatGPT free?
----------------

The simple answer is, yes ChatGPT 4o mini is free. It’s free for personal use and commercial use. Seeing that the tool is a freemium, OpenAI’s strategy is most likely to have as many people use it to obtain more data to fine-tune the model and service.

### ChatGPT Plus

OpenAI has a $20/month paid version, called ChatGPT Plus. Although the functionalities for the Plus plan are similar to the standard free plan, it does come with benefits. You get early access to the latest features, faster response times and you always have access to the chatbot whenever there is peak usage.

### ChatGPT Pro

Besides the Plus plan, OpenAI introduced ChatGPT Pro in December 2024. This subscription is a $200 monthly plan that is more advanced that gives you the highest level of access to the best of OpenAI’s models and tools. This includes unlimited access to GPT-4o and advanced speech, but also the use of to in-depth research tools, the o1 pro mode and the ability to create videos using Sora.

### OpenAI API

Their flagship product is a Pay as You go plan for OpenAI’s API – GPT-4o/GPT-4o mini/GPT-4.1 nano. You simply select a language model that suits your (developer) needs, and pay per 1M tokens used. You can choose between, but are not limited to, GPT-4o/GPT-4o mini/GPT-4.1 nano – which is the most powerful and similar to ChatGPT.

**Note:** Prices for monthly subscriptions in the EU are: GPT Plus – €23 and GPT Pro – €229.

What payment methods are accepted for OpenAi and ChatGPT Plus?
--------------------------------------------------------------

After creating an OpenAI account, users can select “[Payment methods](https://platform.openai.com/account/billing/payment-methods)
” under the Billing tab to add a payment card. Any of the following cards are accepted:

*   Mastercard
*   Visa
*   American Express
*   Discover
*   Diners Club

The same methods are accepted to pay for ChatGPT Plus and Pro.

Are there alternative payment methods available for OpenAI subscriptions?
-------------------------------------------------------------------------

The most common alternative payments for OpenAI subscriptions are PayPal and Bank Transfers. We briefly describe them below.

### Is PayPal accepted to pay for ChatGPT?

Unfortunately, you cannot use PayPal to pay for you subscription. OpenAI does not mention if they have any intention of adding PayPal to the payment methods.

### Can I upgrade to ChatGPT Pro or Plus via Bank Transfer?

Similar to PayPal, you cannot pay for your OpenAI subscription via Bank Transfer. Alternatively, it is possible to link a reloadable virtual card to your account, and top-up the card via Bank Transfers.

How to pay for ChatGPT without a credit card?
---------------------------------------------

We understand that not everybody has access to a credit card and PayPal is not an option. Others may not want to expose their real credit card details in a BETA system due to a possible data breach. In all of these cases our Virtual Cards are the way to go. They do not require a credit check and shield your real card details.

###### [Order your Virtual Card today](https://getsby.com/en/my-account/)

Discover the Power of Getsby Virtual Cards for OpenAI
-----------------------------------------------------

Unlock a smarter, safer way to pay for OpenAI services—whether it’s Chat GPT Pro or the API—with a Getsby Virtual Card. Here’s why it’s the perfect choice for your business or personal use:

*   **Top-Notch Security:** Keep your real credit card details safe. A Getsby Virtual Card acts as a shield, reducing the risk of data breaches or unauthorized charges, so you can focus on what matters.
*   **Effortless Accessibility:** No credit or debit card? No problem. Getsby enables payments for users in regions where OpenAI may decline local cards, making it ideal for non-US users or anyone without traditional payment options.
*   **Smart Spending Control:** Take charge of your budget. Reload your card to cover Chat GPT subscription or API token-based billing, and freeze or unfreeze it anytime to stay in control of your spending.
*   **Versatile Usage:** Beyond OpenAI, your Getsby Virtual Card works across platforms like [Reddit Premium](https://www.reddit.com/premium)
     or [Patreon](https://getsby.com/en/creator-platform/subscribe-to-patreon/)
    , offering unmatched flexibility for all your digital subscriptions.
*   **Enhanced Privacy:** Minimize sharing sensitive financial details with third-party services like OpenAI, keeping your information secure and your transactions discreet.

**Good to Know:** A 2% FX fee applies for non-Eurozone transactions, so ensure sufficient funds to cover fees. Calculate the correct value for your one time use card ([link](https://www.mastercard.us/en-us/personal/get-support/convert-currency.html)
) based on the current Mastercard exchange rates for your payments, including FX costs.

How to use your Getsby Virtual Card?
------------------------------------

Here is a quick step-by-step guide to setup your Getsby prepaid Mastercard for ChatGPT Plus.

1.  Create account on Getsby.com.
2.  Select the Green Card with the preferred balance and to the checkout.
3.  You will receive an access code for your virtual card by email within 2 minutes after your payment has been received.
4.  After activation, you have access to your 16-digit card number to subscribe to OpenAI.
5.  Log into your OpenAI account, go to “Payment Methods” under Billing, and enter the card details.
6.  Confirm the payment for Chat GPT Pro or Plus.

Using a virtual card, ensures your real [credit card number](https://getsby.com/en/debit-and-credit-card-expiry-dates-explained/)
 is secure. You can also setup the card in your OpenAI account to pay for your monthly invoices.

[![Pay for your subscription](https://getsby.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/12/CTA-virtual-prepaid-card-1.png)](https://getsby.com/en/my-account/)

Troubleshooting Payment Issues
------------------------------

In some occasion transactions with your virtual card can be declined.

There are a few common reasons for a card decline:

*   Insufficient funds in the account
*   The billing address does not match the card information or address on file
*   You’re entering incorrect card information

You will be able to retry up to three times before your card is blocked.

Final Thoughts
--------------

OpenAI mainly uses credit and debit cards for payments. This can be difficult for people that have no access to a Visa or Mastercard. While this way might help stop fraud and fits how the company works in the U.S., it leaves out many users worldwide who need easier ways to pay.

For these users, Getsby Virtual Cards offer a good solution. They let people sign up for paid services or use APIs, even in places where local cards often don’t work or alternative payments options are not available.

Online shopping is convenient, but it also comes with some real risks. One of the biggest concerns is protecting your payment information. **Virtual credit cards** can be an innovative solution to that problem. They generate **temporary card numbers** you can use for online purchases. If your number gets compromised, you just delete it and move on.

We’ll take a closer look at how these **digital cards** work, [why using **temporary virtual cards**](https://www.reviano.com/blog/how-to-get-a-temporary-credit-card-number-for-safer-online-shopping.html)
 is one of the most innovative ways to shop online safely, and which **virtual credit card providers in the U.S.** deliver on features, security, and value. Whether you’re trying to avoid fraud, manage those pesky subscription services, or just keep your actual card number private, **a virtual card** can make your online shopping experience much easier.

![What is a Virtual Credit Card](https://www.reviano.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/What-is-a-Virtual-Credit-Card.jpg.webp)

Table of Contents

[Toggle](https://www.reviano.com/blog/best-virtual-credit-cards.html#)

**What is a Virtual Credit Card?**
----------------------------------

A virtual credit card is a randomly generated card number you can use when shopping online or over the phone. Unlike your typical plastic credit card, this unique card is only designed for online and card-not-present transactions and is accessible online.

With a virtual card, you can withdraw money from specific ATMs, shop at your favorite online store, or pay for online services without the risk of fraud or personal data theft. Most virtual payment cards—commonly known as disposable or single-use virtual cards—can only be used once, meaning they become useless after use.

In addition to its added security measures, another brilliant feature of a virtual debit card (or credit card) is that it gives you total control. You can set a spending limit for the card and decide to close or pause the card anytime you wish.

How Does a Virtual Credit Card Work?
------------------------------------

Virtual credit cards (VCCs) are like traditional ones but exist only in digital form. They generate a temporary card number linked to your credit card account so you can make online purchases without exposing your primary card details.

1.  **Card Generation**:  
    When you request a virtual card, your bank or card provider generates a unique 16-digit card number, expiration date, and security code. This information is tied to your primary card or bank account but is separate from your physical card details.
2.  **Single Use or Merchant Specific**:  
    Some virtual cards are single-use only, meaning the card details will be invalid after one purchase. Others are merchant-specific, and you can use the same virtual card for recurring payments (like subscriptions) with the same retailer.
3.  **Linked to Your Account**:  
    The virtual card is linked to your primary credit or debit card account. Any transactions made with the virtual card will be charged to your main account, just like a physical card.
4.  **Extra Security**:  
    Virtual cards add an extra layer of security by masking your actual card details. If a hacker intercepts the virtual card information, they won’t have your physical card number or account. Single-use cards also prevent fraudsters from reusing the stolen details.
5.  **Easy to Use**:  
    Virtual cards can be accessed through your bank’s website or a dedicated financial app. You can copy and paste the card details during online checkout or use them for in-app purchases.

**Best Virtual Credit Card Providers**
--------------------------------------

To help make your selection easy, we have highlighted some of the best virtual credit cards in the United States. As a bonus, we added features to make the comparison as easy as pie.  

### **Wise Virtual Credit Card**

![Wise Virtual Credit Card](https://www.reviano.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Wise-Virtual-Credit-Card.jpg.webp)

Wise is a familiar online payment service provider offering users virtual cards. Its digital card has features that simplify managing and controlling expenses with a few clicks. Plus, it allows you to make online transactions using real-time exchange rates.

Wise allows users to freeze their cards after each use, reducing the possibility of fraud. The card also supports over 50 currencies, which can be converted in real-time and used to spend online, abroad, or in-store without difficulties.

To access a Wise virtual debit card, use the Wise app or website, select the option for “card” from the menu, and then you can get a virtual card instantly.

#### **Features**

*   Its services are open to individuals in over 30 countries.
*   Wise virtual debit card supports many currencies (over 50)
*   Free for customers with personal or business accounts
*   You can shop online with a Wise virtual debit card using a real exchange rate.
*   Supports contactless payment using **Apple Pay**, **Google Pay**
*   You can freeze your Wise virtual card anytime.

URL: [https://wise.com/gb/virtual-card/](https://wise.com/gb/virtual-card/)

### **Privacy.com**

![Privacy com Virtual Credit Card](https://www.reviano.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Privacy-com-Virtual-Credit-Card.jpg.webp)

Privacy.com offers users a temporary card number after a careful internal and third-party audit, making their cards one of the most secure.

The cards issued are free of charge, especially for those who link a bank account to the card. Also, unlike some virtual credit/debit card providers who require compulsory credit checks to get their cards, a credit check isn’t needed to create a privacy.com account. Unfortunately, this card is only eligible for U.S. citizens.

#### **Features**

*   Has the spend limit option
*   Can generate up to 60 cards monthly
*   Personal information stays private every time, thus protecting your money and bank account.
*   Free virtual cards if you link them to your bank account
*   Set a maximum charge limit on each card or lock it to a particular vendor

URL: [https://privacy.com](https://privacy.com/)

### **Open. money**

![Open money Virtual Credit Card](https://www.reviano.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Open-money-Virtual-Credit-Card.jpg.webp)

Open is another online payment platform that offers virtual credit card services (credit or debit cards). With their virtual card, you can easily monitor, control, and manage your online business expenditures.

If you like, you can use their “spend limits” option to manage or keep your budget in check. You can also set alerts to inform you of each transaction made with your card.

To receive Open’s virtual card, you must register and open an account via their website. You can embrace their free or paid plan, which offers more features.

#### **Features**

*   Receive alerts for all out-of-budget expenditures.
*   Monitor spending in real-time
*   Ideal for managing online subscription

URL: [https://open.money/products/virtual-card](https://open.money/products/virtual-card)

### **US Unlocked**

![US Unlocked Virtual Credit Card](https://www.reviano.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/US-Unlocked-Virtual-Credit-Card.jpg.webp)

People who love to shop from different US vendors can use US Unlocked virtual cards to facilitate payment regardless of where they are. You’ll have to register for their service, top up your card with $50, and pay a small charge to use the card for one-time purchases from various online vendors in the U.S.

US Unlocked digital card features a U.S. billing/shipping address that smooths payments to U.S. merchants.

Are you a movie fanatic?

The US Unlocked virtual credit card lets you quickly access popular U.S. streaming services like Netflix, Hulu, and HBO Max. Lastly, US Unlocked offers customers two varieties of cards. The first allows a one-time use, while the second is a merchant-specific card,

#### **Features**

*   It comes with a U.S. billing and shipping address
*   It can be used to access U.S.-only websites
*   Links to [packaging forwarding companies](https://www.reviano.com/blog/best-parcel-forwarding-services.html)
    

URL: [https://www.usunlocked.com](https://www.usunlocked.com/)

### **Payoneer**

![Payoneer Virtual Card](https://www.reviano.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Payoneer-Virtual-Card.jpg.webp)

Payoneer—a popular American financial services company—offers its users access to a virtual card. This card, which has low fees and is accepted in many countries, is ideal for individuals who shop online regularly, especially in stores that accept MasterCard payments.

The Payoneer digital card works like a physical card. When you try to pay for something online, you must enter the virtual card number and other details on the checkout page.

You can get a Payoneer virtual card by filling in your details on their website, after which your application is reviewed before you are given a virtual credit or debit card. You must be an account owner to access the virtual credit card service.

#### **Features**

*   Free with a Payoneer account
*   The application process is smooth
*   Ideal for quick online purchases
*   Accepted in online stores that support MasterCard
*   Digital cards aren’t available for countries like **Russia**, **India**, **the European Union**, **the United Kingdom**, etc.

URL: [https://www.payoneer.com/](https://www.payoneer.com/)

### **Citibank**

![Citibank Virtual Card](https://www.reviano.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Citibank-Virtual-Card.jpg.webp)

Citibank is one of the three banks in the United States that support virtual cards. They offer customers two types of virtual cards: single-use and merchant-locked. The former is designed for one-time use, while the latter allows you to lock the card to the first merchant it’s used with, thus streamlining the payment process and reducing your risk online.

Like some virtual cards, the Citibank virtual credit card has an expiry date and spending limits customization options. The card can make local payments in over 40 countries and 30 unique currencies, making it perfect for globetrotters.

You can use the Citibank virtual credit card for online purchases or reservations, which is available to those who own eligible Citibank cards.

#### **Feature**

*   Fees depend on the type of card you use to generate your virtual card with
*   Limit card use to a few hours or up to two years
*   Set a maximum limit or range for spending.

URL: [https://www.cardbenefits.citi.com/Products/Virtual-Account-Numbers](https://www.cardbenefits.citi.com/Products/Virtual-Account-Numbers)

### **American Express**

![American Express Virtual Card](https://www.reviano.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/American-Express-Virtual-Card.jpg.webp)

American Express answers the prayers of many freelancers, especially those who sweat to get a credit card. This global payment company, which also doubles as a virtual card provider, offers its customers virtual credit card services that make online purchases easy, quick, and enjoyable.

American Express VCC has your back if you are a freelance digital marketer, writer, or programmer looking to buy a product or service online easily. The card adds ease and smoothness to the payment process and allows you to set spending limits to help control spending and reduce use.

To create an American Express virtual credit card, you must create a profile on the American Express Go website. Then, after registration, the card will be sent to you through the **Amex Go** app.

#### **Features**

*   Monitor transactions and control spending
*   Simplifies the payment process
*   Easy to use
*   Reusable
*   It helps protect your credit card account online

URL: [https://www.americanexpress.com/en-us/](https://www.americanexpress.com/en-us/)

### **Blur**

![Blur Virtual Card](https://www.reviano.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Blur-Virtual-Card.jpg.webp)

Are you looking for a virtual card provider with badass security? Or one that’s compatible with all devices? Blur, a popular virtual credit card provider in the United States, ticks all the boxes.

Blur has a virtual card with solid security designed to keep bank transactions or online payments safe and secure. You can use it on various devices, including phones, tablets, PCs, etc., making payments and checkout easy.

Blur allows users to create a new credit card number for each purchase, thus making their transactions more secure. It also has a unique encryption feature. With this feature activated, hackers can’t gather sensitive bank account details.

#### **Features**

*   Compatible with all devices
*   Encrypts passwords
*   It adds ease to the payment process
*   It’s free

URL: [https://www.abine.com/blog/2018/how-to-create-a-virtual-credit-card-part1/](https://www.abine.com/blog/2018/how-to-create-a-virtual-credit-card-part1/)

### **Netspend**

![Netspend Virtual Card](https://www.reviano.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Netspend-Virtual-Card.jpg.webp)

Netspend doubles as an online financial management tool and a virtual card provider. It offers users access to a virtual prepaid card that can be used to make secure payments nationwide. The card it provides can help protect your actual credit or debit card information from hackers.

Netspend allows users to personalize their cards with a unique photo or symbol. With this card, you can generate a virtual card number and access websites that don’t accept typical debit cards.

Whenever a transaction occurs on your Netspend virtual card, you get notified immediately, thus ensuring that you are up-to-date with your expenditure. Plus, you can check your account balance from any location.

#### **Features**

*   Easily customizable
*   You can check your account balance on the go.
*   Can operate a Netspend account in different countries
*   Temporary use
*   Create up to 6 free virtual debit cards

URL: [https://www.netspend.com/prepaid-debit/features/virtual-cards](https://www.netspend.com/prepaid-debit/features/virtual-cards)

### **Card.com**

![card com Virtual Card](https://www.reviano.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/card-com-Virtual-Card.jpg.webp)

Online shopping enthusiasts need a virtual card service to protect their details and handle heavy online shopping activities. Card.com is a card with features that can make your shopping spree hassle-free and enjoyable.

You see, Card.com doesn’t only make online shopping fun. It also makes the process of applying for a virtual card easy. Unlike some other cards, Card.com doesn’t demand a credit check from potential users or charge them a hidden fee.

Also, if you care about your card’s aesthetics, Card.com allows its users to customize their cards, choosing among an array of images and photos. This virtual card company has a feature the early direct deposit feature that makes payment fast.

#### **Features**

*   Zero hidden fees
*   Zero credit checks
*   Integrate easily with your American bank
*   Customizable card design

URL: [https://www.card.com/add-money/virtual-wallet](https://www.card.com/add-money/virtual-wallet)

How To Get a Temporary Credit Card Number for Online Purchases?
---------------------------------------------------------------

Each provider has a different method to generate a temporary virtual credit card. For a better understanding, here is a step-by-step guide on how to get a virtual credit card:

*   Log in to your online bank or card account (website or mobile app).
*   Check if the provider offers virtual credit cards.
*   Generate or request a virtual credit card number by visiting your account settings or card benefit section.
*   Customize or personalize the card based on your transaction needs
*   Select a currency (there are multiple currencies to choose from). You can also decide to make the virtual visa card merchant-specific.
*   Set your card spending limit.
*   Go to the checking page of the online merchant you want to transact with
*   Input your virtual card information and go ahead with the purchase

Who Can Benefit From Virtual Cards?
-----------------------------------

Almost every online shopper can benefit from the immense benefits of a virtual card. Specifically, this card is ideal for those who:

*   Are you seeking the best and easiest ways to manage your online subscriptions?
*   Want absolute control over their online payments and finances
*   Want a card with instant activation
*   Want a card that can help them track cash flow anywhere and anytime
*   Want a card with an extra layer of security
*   Want a card that makes shopping online easy and safe
*   Want an easily customizable card
*   Often, they buy products or services from unfamiliar or strange e-commerce stores.
*   Want a card that can help them efficiently manage their finances
*   Run freelance, digital marketing, blogging, or writing businesses
*   Don’t have a credit card and want to access websites that don’t support debit cards

One of the highlights of a virtual card is that it gives you total control. Unlike physical cards, you can set spending limits (monthly or yearly) and expiry dates or even choose a nickname for the card. You can also decide to close or pause the card anytime you wish.

What Are the Advantages of Using a Virtual Credit Card?
-------------------------------------------------------

Virtual credit cards are a real upgrade from the standard physical card. Their advantages include convenience, fraud protection, spending controls, and subscription management.

For better understanding, we emphasized the benefits below.

### **Convenience**

It takes seven to ten days to receive a new credit card (after approval). However, you can quickly generate a virtual credit card with a few clicks. The best part? They come with a customization option, which allows you to edit the card based on your preferences and financial needs.

You can set spending limits or expiration dates. And in case you suspect fraud, you can freeze or close the card with a few clicks. Now, compare these features with a plastic card that puts your account at risk when stolen, and tell us if a virtual credit card service isn’t one of the best services banks offer.

### **Fraud protection**

A virtual credit card eliminates the chances of being a victim of credit card fraud or identity theft.

_But how?_

Most virtual cards are designed for one-time purchases and subscriptions. So if a hacker gets access to the virtual credit card number, which becomes useless after an online transaction, it’ll be impossible for the individual to infiltrate your account.

Also, if the card becomes compromised, you can easily freeze or close it and reopen a new one via your bank’s website.

### **Spending controls**

Are you an impulsive buyer? Do you spend your money uncontrollably? If so, a virtual card can check your spending habits.

It does this by allowing you to set a spending limit for a particular online purchase you intend to make. With that in place, the merchant cannot charge you more. The spend limit feature will also ensure you don’t waste your budget or cash on unnecessary things.

### **It’s free**

**Bank of America**, **Citibank**, and **Discover** don’t charge for their virtual card. These banks will generate a virtual card number for you free of charge.

### **Subscription management**

Are you subscribed to **Netflix**, **Amazon Prime,** **Hulu**, or **Birchbox**? Do you spend much of your time trying to monitor each subscription? If so, you need a virtual credit card to streamline the process.

Digital cards offer you an easy way to manage all your subscriptions. You can easily manage and monitor each subscription by setting up a digital or virtual card.

You can even see merchants ripping you off your hard-earned money and canceling your subscription (free of charge) without changing your payment details for each merchant.

**What Are the Disadvantages of Having a Virtual Credit Card?**
---------------------------------------------------------------

While virtual credit cards (VCCs) offer enhanced security and convenience, they also have drawbacks. Here are the key disadvantages to consider:

### Limited Issuers

Unlike a physical credit or debit card, which many banks offer in the United States, a virtual card, especially those offered by banks, is scarce. These three banks provide this card, including Bank of America, Discover, and Citibank. Other prominent banks don’t offer this service or have stopped.

### Not all merchants accept virtual credit cards.

A virtual card is ideal for online purchases and card-not-present transactions. But, there are some cases or situations where your virtual card becomes as useless as an ashtray on a motorbike.

Not all merchants accept virtual credit cards, especially businesses that require a physical card for in-person transactions, like hotels, car rentals, or gas stations. Some online retailers may also decline VCCs due to security restrictions.

### Refund and Return challenges

A virtual card makes requesting a refund or returning an item complicated. Most retailers often direct refunds to the account used to make the purchase.

Since your card number is temporary and may be invalid when requesting a refund, it becomes difficult to get a refund or return an item.

### No physical card to use in person

Unlike traditional credit cards, virtual credit cards only exist in digital form. So they’re unsuitable when you must swipe or tap a physical card.

### Recurring payment issues

Since virtual credit cards generate new card numbers for each transaction, they can cause problems with subscriptions or recurring payments. If a subscription service requires the same card number for billing, you may need to update payment details frequently.

### Not compatible with some international transactions

Some international merchants or payment processors may not support virtual credit cards, so you can’t use them for purchases in some regions.

### Less fraud protection than physical credit cards

While virtual credit cards mask your actual card number, some banks may not offer the same level of fraud protection or chargeback rights as physical cards. Check with your provider.

### Expiration issues

Some virtual credit card numbers expire after one use or within a short period. If a merchant processes a delayed charge or requires the same card for verification, an expired VCC can cause payment failures.

Despite these limitations, virtual credit cards are an excellent online security and fraud prevention tool. However, they may not replace traditional credit cards for all transactions.

**Conclusion**
--------------

Virtual credit and debit cards are better alternatives to plastic cards or cash. While virtual and physical cards are almost identical, the former has some disadvantages and an edge over its counterpart.

They offer better security and give users total control of their finances, transactions, and the card itself.

When selecting the ideal virtual card vendor, choose one that matches your business or financial needs. Consider its flexibility, ease of use, spending tracking features, and how easy it is to obtain one from the respective virtual card provider.

![FQA](https://www.reviano.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/faq.jpg.webp)

**Virtual Credit Cards FAQs**
-----------------------------

### Are Virtual Credit Cards Legal?

Yes. Virtual credit cards are entirely legal as they are issued by major banks and companies that are 100% trusted. You can save the card details on your device and use them from anywhere without any hassle. Virtual credit card generators are legal if you aren’t using them for transactions, such as signing up for a free trial.

### Can I Use the Virtual Credit Cards in Stores?

Yes, you can use the virtual card to make contactless payments in stores by integrating it with digital wallets like **Apple Pay, Samsung Pay,** or **Google Pay**.

### Can I Withdraw Cash From ATMs With a Virtual Card?

Yes, the virtual card also allows you to withdraw money or cash from ATMs that support contactless use. You’ll need a digital wallet like **Apple Pay, Samsung Pay,** or **Google Pay** and a virtual card PIN code.

### Which Banks Offer Virtual Credit Cards?

Virtual credit cards are only available to **Citibank**, **Bank of America**, and **Capital One** customers. These banks offer their customers the option directly through the card, a different application (in the case of **Citibank**), or a browser extension.

Although prominent banks like **Discover**, **Chase**, **Bank of America**, and **Wells Fargo** don’t have cards that offer virtual payment services, they have a similar option—digital wallet solutions. A digital wallet lets customers pay for products using their phones instead of cash or a card.

### Does PayPal Offer a Virtual Credit Card?

The short answer is yes. **PayPal** offers a virtual credit card. However, the virtual card is limited to one use only and can’t be used several times. Each time you need a virtual card from **PayPal**, you need to request one, and **PayPal** will offer you the virtual card details, including the card number, CVV, and expiry date, which you need to copy to use for your online purchases.

### **Can I Create a Virtual Credit Card on Google Pay?**

Yes, you can create virtual cards on **Google Pay**. Add a card to your Google account (skip if a credit or debit card has already been added) and ensure the save and fill payment option is on.

You can use them to sign up for free trials without worrying about charges if you forget to cancel before the period ends. * **Single-Use:** These cards automatically cancel themselves once you use them. They’re ideal when you want to ensure a merchant can’t overcharge you. In addition to its impressive virtual card functions, the X1 Visa has decent rewards, especially for a card with no annual fee. You earn two points per dollar spent by default, but it goes up to three points per dollar if you spend more than $15,000 in a year. Notably, the X1 Visa doesn’t require a hard credit check. Instead, the virtual card provider uses your income to determine whether you qualify and set your credit limit. As a result, it’s best for those with limited credit but high earning power. [Try X1 Visa](https://x1creditcard.com/) **Apple Credit Card** --------------------- Unlike most credit cards, the physical [Apple Card](https://www.apple.com/apple-card/financial-health/) shows no information other than your name. You won’t find any card details on its face, so people can’t steal your account number, security code, or expiration date. You can still use it to complete an online purchase with a merchant that accepts Apple Pay. However, the security feature prevents you from providing your account information in the standard checkout format. To get around that issue, the Apple Card lets you generate a virtual credit card number that you can use for online shopping like you would with a traditional credit card that shows its account details. The number lasts indefinitely, but you can request a new one anytime. That way, you can cancel and replace it to protect yourself if a merchant abuses your data or loses it in a breach without waiting for a new physical card to arrive. Unsurprisingly, the Apple Card has a lot to offer beyond its virtual card and security features. That includes: * 3% cash back on Apple Pay purchases at merchants like Apple, Uber, Exxon, and more * 2% back on Apple Pay purchases at all other merchants * 1% back on purchases with the physical Apple Card In addition, Apple Card uses FICO Score 9 to assess your creditworthiness and considers a score above 660 favorable enough to qualify. Since the [average FICO](https://www.experian.com/blogs/ask-experian/what-is-the-average-credit-score-in-the-u-s/) score is 714, it’s accessible to most consumers. For a list on [Second Chance Credit Card with No Security Deposit](https://digitalhoney.money/second-chance-credit-card-with-no-security-deposit/) , check it out for more information on other credit cards available. Citi’s virtual credit card features are more robust than Apple’s. Not only does it let you generate multiple cards at once, but you can also customize each one’s spending limit and expiration date, though customer reviews indicate some interface issues. The [Double Cash card](https://citicards.citi.com/usc/LPACA/Citi/Cards/DoubleCash/ps_A/index.html) is one of Citi’s better options with these features. There’s no annual fee, and the cashback rewards are competitive. They include: * $200 cash back after spending $1,500 in the first six months * 1% cash back when you spend plus 1% when you pay off balances * 0% intro APR on balance transfers for the first 18 months Citi is a traditional credit company and checks your score when you apply. You’ll need a good one to qualify for the Double Cash card, so it’s not a viable option for borrowers with bad credit or limited payment history. [Try Citi](https://citicards.citi.com/usc/LPACA/Citi/Cards/DoubleCash/ps_A/index.html) **Capital One Venture X Rewards Credit Card** --------------------------------------------- Capital One is another major credit card issuer that offers virtual credit cards. Rather than limit you to a single virtual card number at a time like Apple, Eno can generate a new virtual card number for each merchant. You can lock, unlock, and delete them freely once you download the Eno browser extension. The [Venture X](https://www.capitalone.com/credit-cards/lp/venture-x/lp1/) Rewards card is one of Capital One’s best accounts eligible for this capability, boasting top-tier travel rewards. They include: * 75,000 in bonus miles when you spend $4,000 in the first three months * 10,000 bonus miles each year as an anniversary bonus * $300 annual travel credit for bookings through Capital One Travel * $100 credit on Global Entry or TSA PreCheck * 10x miles on hotels and rental cars and 5x miles on flights on Capital One Travel * 2x miles on all other purchases However, there are some significant drawbacks to the account as well. In addition, the card has a whopping $395 annual fee. While you can easily make up for that cost with the card’s lucrative rewards over time, you must be able to afford the expense upfront. It will even provide a projection of future budgets based on data they collect from the past. Their mobile app can also be used with Android and Apple phones and has received excellent reviews from users. Check out the BILL Divvy Corporate Card today! [Try BILL](http://digitalhoney.money/go/divvy) **US Unlocked** --------------- Revolving credit accounts with built-in virtual card number capabilities are typically the best way to protect yourself from being defrauded and overcharged while spending, but they’re not always accessible. If you have a [bad credit score](https://digitalhoney.money/best-secured-credit-card-to-build-credit/) or prefer not to take on an additional credit account, then prepaid debit cards with virtual number capabilities may be a better option. You can also check out our list [Credit Card Alternatives](https://digitalhoney.money/credit-card-alternatives/) if these fees don’t sound good to you. [Try US Unlocked](https://www.usunlocked.com/) **Privacy** ----------- Next up in the prepaid virtual debit card category is [Privacy](https://privacy.com/) . Because it’s for consumers and businesses alike, the tool offers more advanced features than its peers. With it, you can: * Create one-time use and merchant-locked virtual debit cards * Assign separate cards to different departments, projects, or vendors * Set budgets for individual cards and track transactions in real-time Best of all, you can access these features for free with Privacy’s Personal subscription tier. These cost $10 per month for 36 cards and $25 for 60 cards per month, respectively. They also let you earn 1% cashback on eligible transactions totaling up to $4,500 per month. [Try Privacy](https://privacy.com/) **Netspend** ------------ [Netspend](https://www.netspend.com/prepaid-debit) is another prepaid debit card you can use in lieu of a credit card with virtual capabilities. Like the other prepaid cards on this list, you don’t need to undergo a credit check to sign up – just verify your identity. There’s no way to avoid fees with Netspend, but you do have multiple choices of [payment plans](https://www.netspend.com/account/terms/730462222.pdf) . They include: * **Pay-as-you-go:** The default plan includes no monthly fee, but you’ll pay $1.95 per transaction. It’s probably the best choice if you only intend to complete one or two each month. * **Monthly:** If you know you’ll have too many transactions to pay as you go, you can skip the associated fees in exchange for making a $9.95 monthly payment. The cost drops to $5 per month if you connect a direct deposit of at least $500 to the account. As usual, you must load the Netspend account with funds from your bank before spending, which is free. Once you have, you can generate up to 6 temporary virtual account numbers to hide your real credit card information from vendors. As usual, you can also cancel these virtual accounts whenever you want to prevent unwanted charges. [Try Netspend](https://www.netspend.com/prepaid-debit) **Wise** -------- Lastly, we have [Wise](https://wise.com/gb/card/) , formerly known as TransferWise. It’s an online checking account designed to facilitate international money transfers and accommodate multiple currencies without the hassle and costs of a traditional bank account. The Wise account offers a debit card that you can use for your personal or business spending. Once you order one, you can generate a virtual version with a different account number than your physical card. Opening a Wise account is free, but requesting a debit card costs a one-time fee of $9, and you must have one to get a virtual card. There are no costs to spend with the card, but you’ll pay $1.50 plus 2% for each withdrawal after you take out $100 in a month. The costs become more complicated if you have international transactions, but they’re lower than what traditional financial institutions charge. If you deal in multiple currencies, Wise should be one of the virtual cards you consider. [Try Wise](https://wise.com/gb/card/) **Are Virtual Credit Cards Legit?** ----------------------------------- Virtual credit cards are a legitimate security tool that can hide your credit card account numbers and personal information from third parties, stop unwanted vendor charges, and help protect you from credit card fraud. However, they aren’t foolproof. Virtual cards connect to your original credit line, so people with the details can still attempt to overcharge or defraud you. That said, the best virtual cards have features that let you limit the card’s usage to prevent these issues by setting spending limits or automatically canceling them. Even the virtual cards that don’t offer these benefits have value because you can cancel them and get new account details immediately if your information is ever compromised. Otherwise, you’d have to wait seven to ten business days for a new physical credit card to arrive in the mail. **Which Virtual Card Is the Best?** ----------------------------------- The best virtual credit card for you ultimately depends on your credit scores and spending activities. For example, the Capital One Venture X card is probably the most well-rounded option for personal transactions if you have excellent credit. However, the Apple Card might be a better choice if you have an average credit score since they have lower creditworthiness requirements. Meanwhile, The BILL Divvy Corporate Card is probably the best credit card for you if you’re looking for a spend and expense management program for your business that can all be used from an app. Finally, Privacy is probably best for those who don’t want to take on additional credit. Its virtual card capabilities are superior to other prepaid debit cards, and it’s free by default. Historically, new users got free credits when signing up (somewhere around US $5–18) for testing the API. Unfortunately, that free-credit grant has been mostly discontinued.

The “free API key / trial credit” hook that many tutorials mention is often outdated. Many newer users report they never received any credits.

That means in practice, to use the API (even for small tests or proof-of-concepts) you normally need to add a valid payment method.  
Using the API is billed per use — there’s no “always-free forever” plan (at least not officially).

If I were building a small test project today:  
I’d prepare to spend a small amount on prepaid credits (just enough to cover a few dozen or hundred runs).  
I’d start with a simple, small model + minimal token usage + good prompt engineering so I only pay for what’s truly needed.  
I’d track usage carefully — even small experiments add up if you’re sloppy with prompts or output size.

No, OpenAI does not currently offer automatic free trial credits for new accounts.  
You must add a payment method and pay per usage.

However, there are several legitimate programs and alternatives that let you use OpenAI (or other LLMs) for free or at very low cost.

Here are some ways to Get Free or Subsidized Access to OpenAI API:

1.  Data Sharing Program (Up to ~11M free tokens/day)  
    OpenAI offers a Data Sharing Program where organizations can receive significant free usage in exchange for letting OpenAI use their prompts + completions for model improvement.

Key details:

*   Up to 11 million free tokens/day (across GPT-4o, GPT-4o mini, etc.)
*   Must explicitly opt-in through an application
*   Your account must have a positive balance (even a few dollars)
*   Not automatically granted — this is a formal program, not the toggle in account settings  
    This is currently the only way to get large-scale free usage.

2.  Researcher Access Program (Up to $1,000 in API credits)  
    OpenAI supports researchers working on:

*   Responsible AI
*   Societal impacts
*   Safety, ethics, or governance

Eligibility:  
Must be affiliated with a university, research lab, or nonprofit  
Can receive up to $1,000 in free credits  
This is ideal for academic proof-of-concepts or scientific projects.

3.  Startup Programs (VC-backed startups)  
    If your startup is funded by certain venture capital firms, you may qualify for:  
    OpenAI for Startups  
    Available to portfolio companies of VCs like:

*   Sequoia
*   Thrive Capital
*   a16z
*   Khosla
*   and others  
    Some partners offer free OpenAI credits as part of their ecosystem benefits  
    Good option for early-stage founders with VC support.

**Some posts online claim that you can get free credits if you turn on the data “share data to improve models”. But this is incorrect. They won't provide you credits for toggling this option.**

If your goal is just experimenting or building a proof-of-concept, the following services provide free LLM access:

*   OpenRouter (many models free or very cheap)
*   Google Gemini API (free daily limits)
*   Hugging Face

And if you have a very tight budget and can invest a little, then here are some tips for you:

*   Use GPT-4o mini instead of GPT-4o (much cheaper)
*   Set strict max\_tokens and shorten outputs during testing
*   Cache repeated system prompts and responses
*   Stream responses to reduce unnecessary tokens
*   Prototype first using free models from OpenRouter or Gemini

**As of late 2025**:  
• OpenAI no longer hands out automatic free credits just for creating a new API account. You normally have to add a payment method and prepay a small amount before you can call the API. ￼  
• The "free tier" wording you see in docs usually refers to promo / grant credits if you happen to have them, not a standing free plan for everyone. ￼

So if you are just a regular indie dev spinning up a project, assume you will need to drop a few bucks to unlock the API.

Also worth noting: ChatGPT Plus and API billing are separate. Paying for ChatGPT does not give you API credits.

### Legit ways to get actual free credits

These all require fitting certain criteria, but they are real:

a) R**esearcher Access Program (academia / nonprofits)**

If you have an active affiliation with a university, research org, or qualifying nonprofit, you can apply for the OpenAI Researcher Access Program, which gives up to $1,000 in API credits for approved projects. ￼

Good fit if your "test project" can be framed as research on AI safety, societal impact, etc.

b) **OpenAI for Startups (VC-backed startups)**

If you are part of a startup backed by certain VC partners, OpenAI for Startups can provide API credits as part of their program. They ask for a referral code from your VC plus a short description of what you are building. ￼

So this is more for funded teams, not a solo side project, but good to know.

c) **Open source projects - Codex open source fund**

OpenAI runs a Codex Open Source Fund where open source projects can receive up to $25,000 in API credits. ￼

If your test project can be truly open source and interesting enough, this might be an option.

d) **Microsoft for Startups Founders Hub (Azure OpenAI)**

If you are ok using Azure OpenAI instead of direct OpenAI API, the Microsoft for Startups Founders Hub has a tier that includes around $2,500 in OpenAI credits as part of their package. ￼

That is arguably the most realistic "big free credit" route for a scrappy builder who can describe a startup idea.

e) **Event / hackathon credits**

OpenAI sometimes gives out promo credits via partners, hackathons, or events tied to your org ID. ￼

That is more luck / timing based, but if you join official hackathons you can occasionally get a chunk of usage for free.

### If you do pay a little: how to keep it dirt cheap

Assuming you toss in, say, $5–10:

a) **Use the mini models**

Mini models are much cheaper per token than the big flagship ones. Pricing for smaller models (gpt-4o-mini, GPT-5 mini, etc.) is in the ballpark of a tiny fraction of a dollar per million tokens for input, and still low for output, making them several times cheaper than flagship models. ￼

For a small POC, 5–10 dollars on a mini model can go a surprisingly long way.

b) **Keep prompts and responses small**

**A few practical tricks**:  
• Trim context: do not send full documents every call, send IDs or summaries.  
• Cap max\_tokens on responses so the model cannot ramble.  
• Use short system messages and reuse them from your app instead of resending giant prompt templates every time.

c) **Design prompts in ChatGPT first**  
• Iterate on your prompts in the ChatGPT UI until they do what you want.  
• Once the behavior looks good, port that prompt into your API code.  
• This avoids burning API tokens on every "tweak one word, try again" experiment.

d) **Be careful with tools like Web Search**

Tools like web search or very heavy models can be much more expensive per call than plain text completion. If your POC does not need search or vision, keep it simple at first. ￼

e) **Use usage caps and alerts**

**In the OpenAI platform you can**:  
• Set hard and soft usage limits.  
• Monitor per-project usage to see which feature is burning tokens. ￼

If you are worried about surprise bills, this is your safety net.

### "Workarounds" to reduce spend even more

**Nothing shady, just good habits**:  
• Prototype logic with open source models locally (Ollama etc.) where possible, then only switch to OpenAI API once the flow feels right.  
• Add a "model" flag in your app config so you can do:  
• dev: super cheap mini model  
• staging/prod: nicer, larger model when needed  
• Batch offline stuff (like generating a bunch of test data) using the Batch API, which can be discounted compared to normal calls.

Best Credit Cards for Free Trials (And Virtual-Card Alternatives)
=================================================================

April 30, 2026

Free trials are designed to convert. The pattern is familiar: enter a credit card, click "start free trial," forget about it, and 30 days later see a charge for $14.99 or $79 a year. The good news is that the right credit card or virtual-card service makes both signing up and canceling painless. Here is what to use and how to keep auto-renewals from bleeding your bank account.

Why Free Trials Want a Credit Card
----------------------------------

Merchants run a $1 (sometimes $0) authorization hold to verify the card is real and has funds. The hold drops off in a few days. The card stays on file for the eventual recurring charge.

[Debit cards](https://www.firstcard.app/learn/does-a-debit-card-build-credit)
 do not work as well for trials because:

*   The authorization hold ties up real money in your checking account.
*   If the merchant overcharges, you have to chase a refund.
*   Stopping a recurring charge usually requires closing the debit card or going through bank dispute, both of which are slower than using a credit card.

For any free trial that auto-renews, a credit card is almost always the better tool.

What Makes a Credit Card Good for Free Trials
---------------------------------------------

*   **Strong fraud and dispute support.** If the merchant ignores your cancellation, you want a card issuer that quickly reverses the charge via [chargeback](https://www.firstcard.app/learn/credit-card-chargeback)
    .
*   **Easy mobile cancellation alerts.** Some cards let you flag a recurring merchant and notify you before each charge.
*   **[Virtual-card numbers](https://www.firstcard.app/learn/virtual-cards-for-online-payment)
    .** Several issuers allow you to generate a one-time or recurring-merchant-specific card number that masks your real account.
*   **Zero-liability fraud policy.** Federal law caps liability at $50, but most major issuers go to $0.
*   **Cashback or rewards on purchases.** If you do convert, you may as well earn points.

Top Credit Card Picks
---------------------

### Chime Card

The [Chime Card](https://api.creditship.ai/c/chime-card?utm_source=blog&utm_medium=web&utm_campaign=article&utm_content=web%3Acredit-card-for-free-trials%3Atop-credit-card-picks%3A0)
 is our top pick for free-trial sign-ups, and it comes down to how it caps spending. It is a secured card with no preset limit, so you can only spend what you have already moved into the account. Move over a few dollars and a forgotten trial cannot run away with a $79 renewal, the charge simply gets declined. There is no annual fee and no interest (as of June 2026), and Chime reports your payments to all three major bureaus, so any trial you do convert into a real subscription quietly builds your file. You also get a virtual card the moment you are approved, plus a one-tap card lock in the app, so you can freeze the number the second a merchant ignores your cancellation. If you want the full rundown on how Chime's checking, savings, and early-pay features fit around the card, our [Chime review](https://www.firstcard.app/learn/chime-review)
 covers it.

Best for: Everyday credit building

### Revolut

If your main goal is to spin up a fresh card number for every trial, Revolut is the neobank built for it. A free Revolut account lets you create multiple virtual cards (up to 20 active at a time) plus a disposable card whose number refreshes after every purchase, so a merchant never sees a number it can bill twice. You can delete or freeze any virtual card in a couple of taps, which kills a recurring charge instantly without a single support call. Revolut is one of the digital banks that came through the neobank shakeout in good shape, so the feature set is stable. One catch: disposable cards are not meant for recurring billing, so use a regular multi-use virtual card with a low limit for any trial you actually plan to keep.

### Capital One Quicksilver / SavorOne

Virtual-card support through the Eno browser extension. Generate a unique virtual card number per merchant. If a service won't cancel, you simply revoke the virtual card and the charges stop. 1.5%-3% cash back on regular purchases.

**Best for:** people who want a single tool for both real purchases and free-trial sign-ups.

### Citi Double Cash

2% cash back on everything (1% when you buy + 1% when you pay). Citi's virtual card number tool lets you generate a temporary card number for online use. Strong dispute and chargeback process.

**Best for:** earning the most predictable 2% rewards on purchases that do convert from trial.

### American Express Blue Cash Everyday

No annual fee, 3% cash back at U.S. supermarkets, gas, and online retail. Amex's customer service for unauthorized charges is among the best in the industry.

**Best for:** Amex's dispute process and high category bonuses if the trial converts to a recurring service you actually want.

### Discover it® Cash Back

Rotating 5% categories, freeze-on-app feature, free FICO score. The freeze feature lets you instantly stop charges from going through, which doubles as a panic button if a merchant ignores your cancel request.

**Best for:** users who want easy on/off control of the card.

### Chase (via Chase Temporary Card Numbers)

If you have a Chase card, you can generate a [Chase temporary credit card number](https://www.firstcard.app/learn/chase-temporary-credit-card-number)
 for online merchants. Same idea as Capital One's Eno: revoke the virtual number, the charges stop.

For Thinner Credit Files
------------------------

If you do not yet qualify for a major rewards card, starter cards still work for trials. The [Self Visa® Credit Card](https://api.creditship.ai/c/self-visa-credit-card?utm_source=blog&utm_medium=web&utm_campaign=article&utm_content=web%3Acredit-card-for-free-trials%3Afor-thinner-credit-files%3A0)
, [OpenSky](https://api.creditship.ai/c/open-sky?utm_source=blog&utm_medium=web&utm_campaign=article&utm_content=web%3Acredit-card-for-free-trials%3Afor-thinner-credit-files%3A1)
, and [Current Build Card](https://api.creditship.ai/c/current?utm_source=blog&utm_medium=web&utm_campaign=article&utm_content=web%3Acredit-card-for-free-trials%3Afor-thinner-credit-files%3A2)
 all process online charges normally and report to all three bureaus, which builds your file while you sign up for trials.

Best for: Everyday credit building

![](https://www.firstcard.app/assets/icons/info_details.svg)Details

Virtual Card Services (The Most Powerful Tool)
----------------------------------------------

Virtual cards are the cheat code for free trials. They are temporary or merchant-locked card numbers tied to your real card. Two reasons they crush traditional credit cards for trials:

*   **Set spending limits per card.** A trial that says "$0 for 30 days then $19.99" can be capped at $1, so even if you forget to cancel, the recurring charge is declined.
*   **Pause or close the card with one click.** No customer service calls, no merchant negotiation.

The biggest privacy-card service is Privacy.com (free), although Capital One's Eno and Citi's virtual account numbers offer similar functionality if you already have those cards.

A Practical Free-Trial Workflow
-------------------------------

### 1\. Generate a virtual card per trial

Use Privacy.com or Eno to create a unique virtual card number for each free trial. Set a $1 spending limit so any unauthorized charge is declined.

### 2\. Calendar the cancel date

The second you sign up, set a calendar reminder for 2 days before the trial ends. Two days, not the day before. Time-zone math and weekend support gaps cost real money.

### 3\. Cancel a few days early

Most services honor cancellations until the moment the trial ends. Canceling early gives you buffer time if the cancel button does not actually work the first time (a surprisingly common bug).

### 4\. Save the cancellation confirmation

Screenshot the confirmation email or web page. If the merchant charges you anyway, this is your evidence for a chargeback.

### 5\. If charged, dispute fast

Major card issuers will typically reverse a recurring charge if you have proof of cancellation. Call the number on the back of the card, request a chargeback, and provide the screenshot.

What About Prepaid Cards or Debit Cards?
----------------------------------------

[Prepaid cards](https://www.firstcard.app/learn/prepaid-cards-that-build-credit)
 work for some trials but not others. Many merchants reject them because they cannot reliably bill the recurring charge. Streaming services, gym memberships, and software subscriptions usually accept them. Travel sites, insurance, and high-ticket subscriptions often reject them.

Debit cards work but are riskier. The trial holds tie up real money, refunds are slower, and a forgotten cancellation hits your checking account directly.

For zero-stakes trial sign-ups, a virtual card on top of a real credit card is the safest combination.

A Note on "Free Trial" Schemes That Aren't Free
-----------------------------------------------

Some offers labeled as free trials are not. Watch for:

*   "Free trial, $4.95 shipping" then auto-enrollment in a $79/month subscription
*   Trial gateways that require entering details on a page that does not say "trial"
*   Sweepstakes and "risk-free" offers that share your card with multiple merchants

A virtual card with a $1 cap stops most of these in their tracks.

Free Trials and Building Credit
-------------------------------

If you do convert a trial to a paid subscription, putting that recurring charge on a builder card adds steady, predictable activity that reports to all three bureaus. A monthly Netflix charge on a [Self Visa® Credit Card](https://api.creditship.ai/c/self-visa-credit-card?utm_source=blog&utm_medium=web&utm_campaign=article&utm_content=web%3Acredit-card-for-free-trials%3Afree-trials-and-building-credit%3A0)
 or [Current Build Card](https://api.creditship.ai/c/current?utm_source=blog&utm_medium=web&utm_campaign=article&utm_content=web%3Acredit-card-for-free-trials%3Afree-trials-and-building-credit%3A1)
, paid in full each month, is exactly the kind of low-utilization positive history that lifts a thin file. Gamers tend to stack the most recurring subscriptions of all, Game Pass, PlayStation Plus, Discord Nitro, and the occasional trial, so if that describes your spending, our guide to the [best credit cards for gamers](https://www.firstcard.app/learn/credit-card-for-gamers)
 covers cards that reward those charges while you build.

Monitor weekly with [Creditship](https://www.creditship.ai/)
 to confirm the activity is reporting correctly.

Best for: People who need to improve their credit

![](https://www.firstcard.app/assets/icons/info_details.svg)Details

Frequently Asked Questions
--------------------------

### Can I use a virtual credit card for free trials?

Yes, and it is the safest option. Virtual cards from Privacy.com, Capital One Eno, or Citi Virtual Account Numbers let you set spending caps and pause the card any time, which makes auto-renewals harmless.

### Is there a neobank that lets me issue multiple cards for trials?

Yes. Revolut lets you keep several virtual cards active at once and add a disposable card whose number changes after every purchase, which is ideal for one-off trial sign-ups. Chime gives you a virtual card and an instant in-app card lock, and because its secured card has no preset limit, a trial can only ever charge what you moved into the account.

### Will canceling a free trial hurt my credit?

No. Canceling a subscription is not reported to credit bureaus. Only missed payments on credit accounts hurt your score.

### What if a company refuses to cancel my trial?

File a chargeback through your credit card issuer with proof of cancellation. Most major card issuers reverse the charge within 7 to 14 days when the cancel-attempt evidence is strong.

### Can I use a debit card for a free trial?

Yes, but not recommended. Debit cards tie up real money during authorization holds and offer weaker dispute protection than credit cards. Use a credit card or virtual card whenever possible.

The Ultimate Guide to the Top 10 Virtual Credit Cards of 2025
=============================================================

2025-05-19 09:00:00MoreLogin

Virtual credit cards (VCCs) have become essential tools for secure, flexible, and innovative digital payments in 2025. Designed to replace traditional cards, VCCs offer masked numbers, spending limits, multi-currency support, and fraud protection. This guide evaluates the top 10 VCCs based on security, usability, rewards, and innovation.

**Introduction: Why Virtual Credit Cards Are the Future of Payments** 
----------------------------------------------------------------------

Let’s face it: the days of fumbling through your wallet for a physical card are so 2010. In 2025, virtual credit cards aren’t just a trend—they’re a financial survival tool. Imagine paying for your Netflix subscription, booking a last-minute flight, or managing your startup’s expenses without ever exposing your real card number. Sounds like magic? It’s not. It’s the power of virtual credit cards (VCCs)—digital payment tools that generate disposable or reloadable card numbers for secure, flexible transactions.  

### **What exactly are virtual credit cards?** 

Unlike traditional cards, VCCs have no physical form. They’re designed for online and subscription-based spending, offering features like:  

*   **Masked card numbers** to protect your primary account from fraud.  
    
*   **Custom spending limits** to curb overspending.  
    
*   **Multi-currency support** for global transactions (critical for services like ChatGPT Plus or AWS, which often reject domestic cards).  
    

But with dozens of options flooding the market, how do you pick the right one? Don’t worry—we’ve done the legwork. In this guide, we’ll rank the top 10 virtual credit cards of 2025, breaking down their perks, quirks, and who they’re perfect for. Whether you’re a crypto enthusiast, a privacy hawk, or a small business owner, there’s a virtual card with your name on it.  

(Fun fact: Did you know 68% of U.S. consumers now prefer virtual cards for online shopping? Blame it on rising fraud fears or sheer laziness—either way, the future is digital.) 

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXcdIqRWI7NulXp4nuToIlkmIfY8zzbvfyMHKq3K2lOfT4C-UmSXNWmd9vsKkUTyJYr3feJdiKbGneNX3aOzDVi37y5SbZgiW-RRPt7eRfesUZaUbI8vm2sY37Hcwq_k8YCNzKabmw?key=EiOwhkoJujMqDRhbKZjWvqP-)

**What Makes a Great Virtual Credit Card in 2025?** 
----------------------------------------------------

Before we dive into the rankings, let’s talk about the four pillars we used to evaluate these cards:  

### **1\. Security: Your Digital Bodyguard** 

A virtual card without ironclad security is like a bike without wheels—useless. We prioritized cards with:  

*   Military-grade encryption  
    
*   Real-time fraud alerts  
    
*   Custom spending limits (because no one needs a $500 Uber Eats charge at 2 AM).  
    

### **2\. Usability: No PhD Required** 

If you need a 10-step tutorial to generate a card, it’s a hard pass. Top picks had:  

*   Instant card generation (think: under 30 seconds)  
    
*   Seamless app integrations (Slack, QuickBooks, you name it)  
    
*   24/7 customer support that actually answers the phone.  
    

### **3\. Rewards & Fees: More Bang for Your Byte** 

Why settle for a basic card when you could earn cashback, crypto, or even free ChatGPT access? We rewarded cards with:  

*   Transparent fee structures (no hidden “convenience” charges)  
    
*   Subscription perks (hello, free Adobe Creative Cloud)  
    
*   Generous rewards for everyday spending.  
    

### **4\. Innovation: The “Wow” Factor** 

2025’s standout cards don’t just follow trends—they set them. Think AI-powered budgeting, QR code payments, or cards that auto-destruct after one use. 

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXdjf37V7Zv-mJhCh2L7FEQla4bBLxoNAucSVtKw1PkHj-VbFHcLu9fnp7EVdV9H6I2VTitLrtCtccXWnirsvTuhnLiz6J1PjerOhvxdkDZHJ7q-2jXnnrHGeLYDdSii32qzfDc7gQ?key=EiOwhkoJujMqDRhbKZjWvqP-)

**The Top 10 Virtual Credit Cards of 2025** 
--------------------------------------------

Ready to meet the contenders? Let’s count down from 1 to our 10 pick.  

### **1\. Privacy.com: The People’s Champion** 

**Key Features** 

*   **Free tier:** 12 cards/month with basic controls.  
    
*   **Merchant locking:** Cards work only at designated retailers.  
    
*   **1% cashback:** On paid plans ($10/month).  
    

**Best For** 

Personal users and budgeters. [Privacy.com](http://privacy.com/)
’s simplicity and no-nonsense security make it the go-to for everyday spending.  

**Quirks** 

*   No international currency support.  
    
*   Limited business features.  
    

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXcFyz2ssPa3Bwezs59waD-3fyxOSr0xy0ur16BD59pq75fzrDrdysdhUbtEIVWxZ60L_jVtY2RfddT1FHU0si-lXpRF-HtBwPSdwWS1HvhieKeGaSODeY5u1uiIjT2JVUyzYM9Mig?key=EiOwhkoJujMqDRhbKZjWvqP-)

### **2\. Brex: The Startup’s Best Friend** 

**Key Features** 

*   **Unlimited cards:** Scale as your team grows.  
    
*   **Real-time dashboards:** Track spend across departments.  
    
*   **Rewards:** 7x points on rideshares, 4x on travel.  
    

**Best For** 

VC-backed startups and SMEs. Brex’s corporate card perks (e.g., airport lounge access) cater to high-growth teams.  

**Quirks** 

Strict underwriting (rejects high-risk industries like crypto).  ![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXdb73GdhFiqVlIrLgs6nEPcUsH_EXYfCAgBLhvSLXbSs57TrKLA3Tmv4Bl6ppGf07rbxXlkDANWwvon4bwXhczVmUxfSY3lc-6BP8eAapUziBaTPLvAYrc0BDit_xZ2ypasSFth9Q?key=EiOwhkoJujMqDRhbKZjWvqP-)

### **3\. WildCard: The AI Enabler** 

**Key Features** 

*   **ChatGPT Plus optimization:** Auto-approves OpenAI API payments.  
    
*   **No KYC:** Use anonymously with a $100/month limit.  
    

**Best For** 

Global creators and AI enthusiasts. WildCard’s seamless support for services like Midjourney and Claude Pro makes it a fan favorite.  

**Quirks** 

*   Higher fees for non-U.S. transactions (1% + $0.50).  
    
*   Limited customer support.  
    

  

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXdLNAJe68TBz4yM5FxC20__9-VdrbDI03Nbhje9PYoLIWTyqnx3QfoXLi8wZDy_dD_c87iNRPR3jNKOAn_OdaTj60k4Bfl90g_XUFRFbFy78l8zBWLu1qxAAwasN_JPSwCF9CLX9w?key=EiOwhkoJujMqDRhbKZjWvqP-)

### **4\. Ramp: The Automation Guru** 

**Key Features** 

*   Slack integration: Approve expenses in real time via chat.  
    
*   AI-driven insights: Flags duplicate charges or unused subscriptions.  
    
*   Free tier: 1.5% cashback on all spend.  
    

**Best For** 

Startups scaling rapidly. Ramp’s automated workflows cut approval times by 70%, according to user reviews.  

**Quirks** 

*   Steep learning curve for accounting integrations.  
    
*   Requires a business EIN. 
    
    ![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXcwirinsQ15_ryQh0HRUIGC0CZtg5ljDm8hnzhCA4pEag96eAl-nLRDyqIyBsvxAF_pTrzWyoPAwtKTXBae8i8OmH6f9uuS24mjBYEA-hNZ3p8SIB7f3Vzz3yaK5is4kpIAONgJ?key=EiOwhkoJujMqDRhbKZjWvqP-)
    

### **5\. Blur (by Abine): The Privacy Ninja** 

**Key Features** 

*   **Masked cards:** Generate burner numbers that hide your real details.  
    
*   **Ad-blocking suite:** Blocks trackers and spam my pop-ups.  
    
*   **Premium plans:** Unlimited cards for heavy users.  
    

**Best For** 

Anonymous shoppers and privacy advocates. Buying a VPN or donating to a cause? Blur keeps your identity under wraps.  

**Quirks** 

*   No rewards program.  
    
*   Limited customer support hours. 
    
    ![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXdepLY7pvs_M8Rhc8YxvpYkljD3IxyKqOUkxtf4rO7Pkg2I-97wvgHHU-PBJN5fnt05Mj913U41DW4XZmjc4rkcvnlrve3AxX6Kk7Zh-tz9hNhsWMG6LAg0OwLwhW7Cm4jeXxrrxw?key=EiOwhkoJujMqDRhbKZjWvqP-)
    

### **6\. Divvy: The Subscription Whisperer** 

**Key Features** 

*   **Budget-driven cards:** Allocate funds to teams or projects (e.g., $500/month for software tools).  
    
*   **Auto-reset budgets:** Prevent overspending with monthly limits.  
    
*   **Zero fees:** No annual or hidden charges.  
    

**Best For** 

Companies drowning in SaaS subscriptions. Divvy’s shared-balance model lets you maximize cash flow without pre-funding individual cards.  

**Quirks** 

*   Clunky interface for non-U.S. users.  
    
*   Requires employer approval for employee cards.
    
    ![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXeDPNNj0LqVKf8pWiK1k0PVe_4k-I0arrPLUzBD_h6Z0ggV8lKH-7vylLDiCv215vBM0Gv4UnH4-_-UggLWtEf6bWEqKeKtP4StX0JBt7Ks0_X5SLF96_m92CEr2jJloAh529Un?key=EiOwhkoJujMqDRhbKZjWvqP-)
    

### **7\. BILL Spend & Expense: The Fraud Fighter** 

**Key Features** 

*   **Chrome extension:** Generate cards instantly while shopping online.  
    
*   **Real-time alerts:** Get SMS or email notifications for every transaction.  
    
*   **Merchant locking:** Restrict cards to specific vendors (e.g., lock a card to Adobe for Creative Cloud subscriptions).  
    

**Best For**

How AI Tools Are Coded by Credit Card Networks
----------------------------------------------

‍

This is where things get tricky. Based on merchant category codes (MCCs), here's how major AI platforms typically process:

‍

### AI and Productivity Tools:

‍

*   **ChatGPT Plus/Pro** - Codes as 5734 (Computer Software Stores), processed as a software purchase

*   **Midjourney** - Codes as 5734 or 7372 (Computer Programming), processed as software/service

*   **Claude Pro** - Codes as 5734 (Computer Software Stores), processed as a software purchase

*   **GitHub Copilot** - Codes as 5734 or 7372, processed as software/development

‍

### Traditional Software:

‍

*   **Adobe Creative Cloud** - Codes as 5734, processed as a software subscription

*   **Microsoft 365** - Codes as 5734, processed as a software subscription

‍

### Streaming Services (for comparison):

‍

*   **Netflix** - Codes as 4899 (Cable/Streaming), processed as a streaming service

‍

**Key Insight:** Most AI tools code as "Computer Software Stores" (MCC 5734) rather than "Streaming Services" (MCC 4899). This means cards optimized for streaming _likely won't_ give bonus rewards on ChatGPT or Midjourney.

5 Mistakes to Avoid When Using Cards for Subscriptions
------------------------------------------------------

‍

### 1\. Assuming All Software Is Treated Equally

‍

Just because your card offers "streaming rewards" doesn't mean AI tools qualify. Always test new subscriptions or check with your issuer about specific merchant codes.

‍

### 2\. Ignoring Annual Fee Math

‍

A $95 annual fee sounds steep until you realize you're earning $400+ in rewards. Calculate your annual return: (Monthly SaaS spend × reward rate × 12) - annual fee = net value.

‍

Example: $300/month SaaS × 4% rewards × 12 months = $144/year. After $95 fee, you're up $49.

‍

### 3\. Letting Subscriptions Auto-Renew Without Review

‍

The average person wastes $133 monthly on unused subscriptions. Before chasing rewards, audit your active services. Cancel what you don't use.

‍

### 4\. Missing Welcome Bonuses

‍

Most premium cards offer 50,000-100,000 bonus points worth $500-$1,000 after hitting minimum spend. Time new card applications before large annual renewals (like Adobe subscriptions) to easily hit thresholds.

‍

### 5\. Not Using Virtual Cards for Free Trials

‍

If a free trial requires a credit card, use a virtual card number (available through most issuers) to prevent unwanted charges if you forget to cancel.

The Future: Will Cards Create AI-Specific Categories?
-----------------------------------------------------

‍

As AI tool spending explodes—ChatGPT reportedly has 100+ million weekly users paying $20-200/month—credit card issuers are taking notice. We may soon see:

‍

*   Dedicated "AI Services" bonus categories

*   Partnerships between AI platforms and card issuers (similar to Spotify + Amex deals)

*   Higher multipliers on "productivity tools" as a defined category

‍

Until then, your best strategy is choosing cards with broad "software & cloud" language in their bonus category definitions.

Frequently Asked Questions
--------------------------

‍

### Do I need a business credit card for software subscriptions?

‍

No, but business cards often offer better rewards for software spending (like Amex Business Gold's 4X points). If you're a freelancer, consultant, or entrepreneur paying for tools yourself, business cards can make sense. You typically need to report business income, though many sole proprietors use their SSN instead of an EIN.

‍

### Will my credit score be affected by having multiple cards?

‍

Potentially, but not the way you might think. Opening new cards causes a temporary dip (5-10 points) due to hard inquiries, but having more available credit can improve your utilization ratio long-term. The key: never carry balances above 30% of your limit on any single card.

‍

_Note: Kudos credit ranges are a variation of FICO® Score 8, one of many types of credit scores lenders may use when considering your credit card application._

‍

### Can I change my subscription payment method mid-cycle?

‍

Yes, almost all services (Netflix, ChatGPT, Adobe, etc.) allow you to update payment info in account settings. Changes typically take effect at your next billing date. This lets you switch to a better rewards card anytime.

‍

### Are there any cards that specifically reward AI tool purchases?

‍

Not yet. As of 2025, no major issuer has created an "AI tools" bonus category. Your best bet is cards with broad "software & cloud services" definitions (Amex Business Gold) or high flat-rate cash back (Spark Cash Plus).

‍

### What's the best single card if I can only choose one?

‍

For simplicity: Capital One Savor (personal, $0 fee, 3% on streaming/entertainment) or U.S. Bank Triple Cash (business, $0 fee, $100 software credit + 2-3% back). Both offer strong returns without annual fees or complex category tracking.

Making Your Choice: A Quick Decision Framework
----------------------------------------------

‍

*   **Pick Blue Cash Preferred if:** You spend $200+ monthly on traditional streaming (Netflix, Hulu) _and_ want 6% back on groceries too.

*   **Pick Citi Custom Cash if:** Your monthly software spend is exactly $500 or less and you want maximum cash back (5%) with zero annual fee.

*   **Pick Amex Business Gold if:** You're spending $1,000+ monthly on AI tools and SaaS as a business, and you value flexible points over cash back.

*   **Pick U.S. Bank Triple Cash if:** You want a business card with a guaranteed $100 annual credit toward QuickBooks, FreshBooks, or similar services, plus solid ongoing rewards.

*   **Pick Capital One Spark Cash Plus if:** You're managing a team and want 2% back on everything with no category limits, and you spend enough ($150K+/year) to justify the $150 fee.

Conclusion: Turn Every Subscription Into Savings
------------------------------------------------

Your monthly software and AI tool subscriptions aren't going away—if anything, they're only increasing as new productivity platforms launch. The difference between earning nothing and earning 2-6% back on these purchases adds up to hundreds or even thousands of dollars annually.

‍
